rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Raymond Interior Systems-North'S Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, January 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1, Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel production of Raymond Interior Systems-North¿s custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and request for production of documents is granted. The documents and information sought are relevant and defendant failed to demonstrate that any of the documents and information sought are duplicative of prior testimony of defendant¿s witness. The court is not limiting the scope of the deposition and document production beyond that stated in plaintiffs¿ deposition notice. The deposition must occur by January 13. The discovery cut-off date as to this deposition only is extended to January 13 to allow this deposition to occur. Plaintiffs¿ request for monetary sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:00am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE KIRAS et al VS. AMCORD, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Defendant Hill Brothers Chemical Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Precluding The Deposition Of Hill Brothers Custodian Of Records And Person Most Qualified
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, January 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3, Defendant Hill Brothers Chemical Co.¿s motion for protective order precluding the deposition of its custodian of records/person most qualified is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate good cause to preclude the deposition in its entirety, as required by C.C.P. 2025.420. Defendant did not demonstrate that the deposition of its representative in this case would be duplicative of any prior testimony and document production. In order for defendant to prepare their person most knowledgeable, though, it is ordered that plaintiffs must identify the jobsites in Sacramento while employed by Ted J. Cook Painting Co. between 1965-1975 and while employed by the County of Sacramento 1975-1995 and what products were used. Defendant¿s and plaintiffs¿ requests for sanctions are denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:00am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Motion To Quash Notice And Stay Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, January 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Kaiser Foundation Hospitals¿ motion for protective order and motion to quash and stay deposition are denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate good cause to preclude the deposition of Ardella Robinson. Both defendant¿s and plaintiffs¿ requests for sanctions are denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:00am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
